summary: Keep the accurate index pointer unchanged and replace thumb-open clicking with an armed middle-thumb contact.
scope: Preserve the existing indexTip mapping, smoothing, 4pt deadzone and jump rejection.
Allow middle-finger and thumb motion after pointer activation while requiring the index, ring and little-finger control geometry.
Arm after middle and thumb remain separated for 100ms, then click after 80ms contact at the frozen pointer location.
Show a second yellow marker at the middle-thumb contact and require release before another click.
validation: swift test passed 31 tests including click arming, contact activation, pointer continuity and one-shot release behavior
TypeScript typecheck, 32 Web regression tests and Swift warnings-as-errors build passed
Release build, strict codesign and installed 0.8.2 build 16 checks passed
Installed UI confirmed the middle-thumb instruction, permissions remained allowed and MediaPipe GPU reached 26 FPS at 14.0ms
notes: The modifier-free private Core Graphics click source from v0.8.1 is retained.
Final YouTube contact ergonomics remain a physical-hand acceptance test.
